name: Apollo 11
first: First humans on another celestial body
description: The mission that fulfilled Kennedy's 1961 commitment to put a man on the Moon by the end of the decade. Armstrong and Aldrin spent 21 hours 36 minutes on the lunar surface — 2.5 hours of EVA, 21.55 kg of samples, and a single commemorative plaque. The laser retroreflector they installed at Mare Tranquillitatis still receives pulses today, measuring the Earth-Moon distance to millimetre precision. The mission was watched live by ~600 million people, one fifth of humanity at the time.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Saturn V (AS-506) from Kennedy Space Center LC-39A 1969-07-16 13:32 UTC.

events.label: TLI
events.note: Trans-lunar injection burn — S-IVB stage propels CSM/LM toward Moon.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Lunar Module Eagle lands at Mare Tranquillitatis 1969-07-20 20:17:40 UTC.

events.label: FIRST STEP
events.note: Armstrong steps onto lunar surface; 'one small step for [a] man'.

events.label: ASCENT
events.note: Eagle lifts off after 21h 36m on surface.

events.label: SPLASHDOWN
events.note: CM Columbia returns crew to Pacific Ocean 1969-07-24.
